apps.name: Property Manager V1
apps.summary: Maintenance command center for small landlords: intake, dispatch, updates, and issue history.
apps.reviewGate: Beta candidate with M1-M4 complete. Remaining gate is M5 hardening for Jeff test review before claiming a stronger private-beta-ready state.
apps.nextStep: Send Claude/Elon into M5 hardening for Jeff test gate, then keep pushing deployment, storage, onboarding, broader QA coverage, and workflow polish.
notes: Mario: the right wedge is a maintenance command center, not an all-in-one property management suite.
Barry Bot: Core multi-role workflow is live: operator flows, tenant submission/timeline/photos, vendor dispatch/queue/detail, and credential-based auth are in place.

Barry Bot: Current gate is no longer the auth-boundary harness; Bob fixed the hard-coded-port timeout issue in commit 06a6e79 and the teardown hang in commit aac70e0, so auth regression coverage now passes and exits cleanly.
Barry Bot: Product direction is now cleaner: operator pays, tenant/vendor access is free, invite-based onboarding is the path, and regions/urgency/expected completion/import-export are in scope.

Barry Bot: Latest signal: M4 cleared in commit 7c87310 with reports/history shipped across /reports, unit history, property stats, dashboard Done count, Reports nav, and expanded reporting/history queries. State is now M1-M4 complete; only M5 remains for Jeff-gate hardening.

apps.name: Realtor Aid

apps.reviewGate: Needs Jeff QA after the first real build.
apps.nextStep: Keep building the first real version.
notes: Barry Bot: Sim approved Realtor Aid for complete build.
Barry Bot: Jeff is the explicit post-build test gate for this app.
Barry Bot: promoted to one of the two clearest active app tracks.
Elon: this is worth pursuing now as the main realtor build.

apps.name: Clipboard Workflow Utility
apps.summary: Role-specific clipboard workflow utility for prosumers; now reframed as Windows-first, with Chrome later as a secondary surface.
apps.reviewGate: No review gate yet; this is not the active build.
apps.nextStep: Hold until the main app tracks need attention.
notes: Barry Bot: kept active, but no longer framed as the top visible app track while realtor and property-manager work are being prioritized.

Elon: Windows-first plan uses a WPF desktop shell with text-only clipboard listener, SQLite persistence, and a basic recent-history loop. Chrome should be a later phase, not the primary product surface.
